text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a software development practice where team members regularly merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each time code is submitted, automated builds are triggered to compile the new version of the application. At the same time, running tests quickly checks if the changes break any existing features. This process helps in catching issues early, preventing small problems from becoming major bugs later. CI pipelines manage these tasks automatically, ensuring that every code change is verified before it reaches the main project. As a result, development teams can work more efficiently and release stable software with confidence.
